UAObjectType.Description: After completing a unit procedure (e.g. mashing, lautering) on a brewing unit, a UnitProcedureEvent is generated, which contains information such as the procedure name, start and end timestamps and involved unit or material references.

Extension.uawsbrew:ImplementationNote: UAWSBasis:EndTime: This point is the end time of an event. This time is recorded and documented at the end of an event. The end time is determined by the state change “Running” to “Completing”.

UAVariable.Description: This point is the end time of an event. This time is recorded and documented at the end of an event. The end time is determined by the state change “Running” to “Completing”.

UAObjectType.Description: After the execution of an operation within a unit procedure (e.g. heating, mixing, adding ingredients), an OperationEvent is generated. It includes details such as operation name, target parameters and start/end times.

Extension.uawsbrew:ImplementationNote: UAWSBasis:Endtime: This point is the end time of an event. This time is recorded and documented at the end of an event. The end time is determined by the state change “Running” to “Completing”.

UAVariable.Description: This point is the end time of an event. This time is recorded and documented at the end of an event. The end time is determined by the state change “Running” to “Completing”.

UAObjectType.Description: This Objecttype defines the type of tanks used in the brewing process. A tank is a container used to store, process, or transport liquids or gases. In brewing, tanks are typically used for holding water, raw materials, finished products, or waste. Some tanks may include features like heating, cooling, or mixing.

Extension.uawsbrew:ImplementationNote: UAWSBasis:Pressure: This data point gives the current pressure.
UAWSBasis:FillLevel: This data point gives the fill level (degree of filling) of an equipment e.g. a vessel, tank etc.
UAWSBasis:Temperature: This data point gives the current temperature.

UAWSBasis:OperatingMode: The operating mode provides information about the nature and extent of the intervention on the control equipment by the operators, and also via feedback from the equipment (DIN 19 237).

UAWSBasis:PackMLBaseStateMachine: The PackMLBaseStateMachine maps the state of a WS Machine according to the PackML state machine. The PackML state machine is extended to include sub-states of the Weihenstephan Standards.

UAObjectType.Description: This ObjectType defines the type of silos used in the brewing process. A silo is a large container used to store bulk solid materials. In brewing, silos are typically used for holding raw materials such as malt, grain, or other dry ingredients.

UAObjectType.Description: This ObjectType defines the type of malt silos used in the brewing process. A malt silo is a storage container specifically designed to hold malted grain before it is processed in brewing.

UAObjectType.Description: This ObjectType defines the type of malt mills used in the brewing process. A mill is a machine used to crush or grind malted grain into grist, which is essential for mashing. Brewing mills may include features like adjustable rollers, feed control, and dust collection to ensure consistent milling quality and efficiency.

UAWSBrew:QuantityBrewingWater: This data point defines the used quantity of brewing water in process cells.
UAWSBrew:QuantityCereal: This data point defines the used mass quantity of cereal per batch, brewhouse line, process cell, timeframe.
UAWSBrew:QuantityDust: This data point defines the quantity of removed dust during malt/cereal transport/cleaning/processing.
UAWSBrew:QuantityExtract: This data point defines the quantity of extract per batch, brewhouse line, process cell, timeframe.
UAWSBrew:QuantityGrist: This data point defines the quantity of produced grist after milling.
UAWSBasis:Throughput: This data point gives the current throughput in a unit/process cell.

UAWSBasis:Temperature: This data point gives the current temperature.

UAObjectType.Description: This ObjectType defines the type of mash tuns used in the brewing process. A mash tun is a vessel where milled malt is mixed with water and heated to convert starches into fermentable sugars. Mash tuns may include features like agitation, temperature control, and filtration systems to optimize the mashing process.

Extension.uawsbrew:ImplementationNote: UAMachineryEM:Electricity: This data point gives the consumption of electricity.
UAWSBrew:Steam: This data point gives the amount of steam which has been used.
UAWSBrew:HotWater: This data point gives the amount of hot water which has been consumed.
UAWSBrew:FossilFuel: This data point gives the consumption of fossil fuels.
UAWSBrew:Additive: This data point gives the consumption of the main additive which have been used.
UAWSBasis:pHValueProduct: This data point gives the current pH-value of the product during an operation/unit procedure.

UAObjectType.Description: This ObjectType defines the type of silos used for storing spent grain in the brewing process. A spent grain silo is a container designed to collect and temporarily store the solid byproducts left after mashing.

UAWSBrew:CompressedAir: This data point gives the consumption of compressed air.
UAWSBrew:QuantityWashableExtract: This data point defines the lost quantity of washable extract per batch, brewhouse line, process cell, timeframe.

UAObjectType.Description: This ObjectType defines the type of underbacks used in the brewing process. An underback is a vessel positioned under the mash tun or lauter tun to collect wort during lautering. Underbacks may include features like level measurement, heating, and pumping to facilitate wort transfer to the next brewing stage.

UAObjectType.Description: This ObjectType defines the type of wort kettles used in the brewing process. A wort kettle is a vessel where the wort is boiled, usually with hops, to sterilize the liquid and extract flavors and bitterness. Wort kettles may include features like heating systems, agitation, and steam jackets to control boiling and evaporation.

UAWSBrew:QuantityHops: This data point defines the used mass quantity of hops per batch, brewhouse line, process cell, timeframe.
UAMachineryProcessValues:ProcessValueSetpoint: This data point gives the set position of the steam damper in heating facilities, e.g. the vapor valve in an oven.

UAWSBasis:OperatingMode: The operating mode provides information about the nature and extent of the intervention on the control equipment by the operators, and also via feedback from the equipment (DIN 19 237).

UAWSBasis:PackMLBaseStateMachine: The PackMLBaseStateMachine maps the state of a WS Machine according to the PackML state machine. The PackML state machine is extended to include sub-states of the Weihenstephan Standards.
UAMachineryEM:Electricity: This data point gives the consumption of electricity.
UAWSBrew:Steam: This data point gives the amount of steam which has been used.
UAWSBrew:HotWater: This data point gives the amount of hot water which has been consumed.
UAWSBrew:FossilFuel: This data point gives the consumption of fossil fuels.
UAWSBrew:Additive: This data point gives the consumption of the main additive which have been used.
UAWSBrew:QuantityHotWort: This data point defines the quantity of hot wort, leaving the whirlpool unit.
UAWSBrew:QuantityCondensate: This data point defines the quantity of condensate produced during thermal processes.
UAWSBrew:QuantityUnboiledWort: This data point defines the quantity of wort before the cooking process.
UAWSBrew:ExtractCastWort: This data point gives the amount of extract in the cast wort.
UAWSBrew:ExtractUnboiledWort: This data point gives the amount of extract in the unboiled wort.
UAWSBasis:pHValueProduct: This data point gives the current pH-value of the product during an operation/unit procedure.

UAObjectType.Description: This ObjectType defines the type of vapor condensers used in the brewing process. A vapor condenser is a device that cools and condenses steam or vapor produced during wort boiling or other heating steps, converting it back into liquid.

UAObjectType.Description: This ObjectType defines the type of energy storage vessels used in the brewing process. An energy storage vessel stores thermal energy, such as hot water or steam, to optimize energy use and supply during brewing. These vessels may include insulation, pressure control, and temperature monitoring to maintain energy efficiency and safety.

Extension.uawsbrew:ImplementationNote: UAWSBrew:QuantityHotWater: This data point defines the used quantity of hot water in process cells.
UAWSBrew:TemperatureTop: This data point gives the current temperature in the upper zone of a vessel.
UAWSBasis:FillLevel: This data point gives the fill level (degree of filling) of an equipment e.g. a vessel, tank etc.
UAWSBrew:TemperatureBottom: This data point gives the current temperature in the bottom zone of a vessel.

UAObjectType.Description: This ObjectType defines the type of whirlpool vessels used in the brewing process. A whirlpool is a vessel where wort is spun to separate solids like hop residues and trub from the liquid after boiling.

Extension.uawsbrew:ImplementationNote: UAMachineryEM:Electricity: This data point gives the consumption of electricity.
UAWSBasis:Turbidity: This data point gives the current turbidity of a suspension or a product.
UAWSBrew:ExtractLastRunnings: This data point gives the amount of extract in the last runnings.
UAWSBasis:Temperature: This data point gives the current temperature.
UAWSBrew:QuantityCastWort: This data point defines the quantity of cast wort, leaving the wort kettle unit.
UAWSBrew:CountTotalBatches: This data point gives the counter value of the total amount of produced batches.
UAWSBrew:CounterdeficientBatches: This data point gives the counter value of the total amount of deficient batches. Deficient batches do not apply to the companies’ quality specifications.
UAWSBrew:TotalQuantity: This data point counts the total product quantity of a process cell.
UAWSBasis:VolumeFlow: This data point gives the current volume flow.

UAWSBrew:MicrobiologyTestResult: This data point implies the microbiological test result of a batch or product. Bits or integer must be used for batches with the following batch quality states: Table 8: Coding of microbiological test results Microbiology Test Results Integer coding Undefined/Blank 0 Negative 1 Within normal limits 2 Positive 4

UAObjectType.Description: This ObjectType defines the type of mash filters used in the brewing process. A mash filter is a device that separates the liquid wort from the spent grain by pressing the mash through filter plates or membranes.

Extension.uawsbrew:ImplementationNote: UAMachineryEM:Electricity: This data point gives the consumption of electricity.
UAWSBrew:CompressedAir: This data point gives the consumption of compressed air.
UAWSBrew:CO2: This data point gives the consumption of CO2.

UAWSBrew:CountBatchesperFilterCloth: This data point gives the accumulated value of batches running through filter plates/sheets. This point helps to determine the lifetime of filter sheets/cloth as a measure for preventive maintenance.
UAWSBasis:Temperature: This data point gives the current temperature.
UAWSBrew:ExtractFirstWort: This data point gives the amount of extract in the first runnings.
UAWSBrew:ExtractLastRunnings: This data point gives the amount of extract in the last runnings.
UAWSBasis:Turbidity: This data point gives the current turbidity of a suspension or a product.
UAWSBrew:QuantityFirstWort: This data point defines the quantity of first wort during the lautering process.
UAWSBrew:QuantityLastRunnings: This data point defines the quantity of last runnings after the finished lautering process.
UAWSBrew:ContactPressure: This data point gives the current contact pressure of (mash) filter plates during the closing, seal-off and production unit procedure.

UAWSBrew:CountVolumeFilterSheets: This data point gives the accumulated volume of product running through filter plates/sheets. This point helps to determine the lifetime of filter sheets/cloth as a measure for preventive maintenance.
UAWSBasis:VolumeFlow: This data point gives the current volume flow.
UAWSBrew:TemperatureSpargingWater: This data point gives the current sparging water temperature.
UAWSBrew:QuantitySpargingWater: This data point defines the used quantity of sparging water in the lauter tun.

This data point gives the consumption of CO2.

Extension.uawsbrew:ImplementationNote: UAWSBrew:CountBatchesperFilterCloth: This data point gives the accumulated value of batches running through filter plates/sheets. This point helps to determine the lifetime of filter sheets/cloth as a measure for preventive maintenance.
UAWSBasis:Temperature: This data point gives the current temperature.
UAWSBrew:ExtractFirstWort: This data point gives the amount of extract in the first runnings.
UAWSBrew:ExtractLastRunnings: This data point gives the amount of extract in the last runnings.
UAWSBasis:Turbidity: This data point gives the current turbidity of a suspension or a product.
UAWSBrew:QuantityFirstWort: This data point defines the quantity of first wort during the lautering process.
UAWSBrew:QuantityLastRunnings: This data point defines the quantity of last runnings after the finished lautering process.
UAWSBrew:ContactPressure: This data point gives the current contact pressure of (mash) filter plates during the closing, seal-off and production unit procedure.

UAWSBrew:CountVolumeFilterSheets: This data point gives the accumulated volume of product running through filter plates/sheets. This point helps to determine the lifetime of filter sheets/cloth as a measure for preventive maintenance.
UAWSBasis:VolumeFlow: This data point gives the current volume flow.
UAWSBrew:TemperatureSpargingWater: This data point gives the current sparging water temperature.
UAWSBrew:QuantitySpargingWater: This data point defines the used quantity of sparging water in the lauter tun.

UAVariable.Description: This data point gives the accumulated value of batches running through filter plates/sheets. This point helps to determine the lifetime of filter sheets/cloth as a measure for preventive maintenance.

UAObject.Description: This data point gives the current temperature.
This data point gives the amount of extract in the first runnings.
This data point gives the amount of extract in the last runnings.
This data point gives the current turbidity of a suspension or a product.
This data point defines the quantity of first wort during the lautering process.
This data point defines the quantity of last runnings after the finished lautering process.
This data point gives the current contact pressure of (mash) filter plates during the closing, seal-off and production unit procedure.

This data point gives the accumulated volume of product running through filter plates/sheets. This point helps to determine the lifetime of filter sheets/cloth as a measure for preventive maintenance.

This data point defines the used quantity of sparging water in the lauter tun.

Extension.uawsbrew:ImplementationNote: UAWSBasis:OperatingMode: The operating mode provides information about the nature and extent of the intervention on the control equipment by the operators, and also via feedback from the equipment (DIN 19 237).

UAWSBasis:PackMLBaseStateMachine: The PackMLBaseStateMachine maps the state of a WS Machine according to the PackML state machine. The PackML state machine is extended to include sub-states of the Weihenstephan Standards.

UAObject.Description: The operating mode provides information about the nature and extent of the intervention on the control equipment by the operators, and also via feedback from the equipment (DIN 19 237).
The PackMLBaseStateMachine maps the state of a WS Machine according to the PackML state machine. The PackML state machine is extended to include sub-states of the Weihenstephan Standards.

UAObjectType.Description: This ObjectType defines the type of lauter tuns used in the brewing process. A lauter tun is a vessel where the mash is separated into clear wort and spent grains.

Extension.uawsbrew:ImplementationNote: UAMachineryEM:Electricity: This data point gives the consumption of electricity.

UAWSBasis:pHValueProduct: This data point gives the current pH-value of the product during an operation/unit procedure.
UAWSBrew:LauteringDeepCut: This data point gives the counter value of the amount of conducted deep cuts during a lautering unit procedure.

UAWSBrew:QuantityLastRunnings: This data point defines the quantity of last runnings after the finished lautering process.

UAWSBrew:RevolutionsTool: This data point gives the current revolution speed of the tool.

UAWSBrew:QuantityWashableExtract: This data point defines the lost quantity of washable extract per batch, brewhouse line, process cell, timeframe.

UAObjectType.Description: This ObjectType defines the type of wort coolers used in the brewing process. A wort cooler rapidly reduces the temperature of boiled wort to fermentation temperature.

Extension.uawsbrew:ImplementationNote: UAMachineryEM:Electricity: This data point gives the consumption of electricity.
UAWSBrew:SterileAir: This data point gives the amount of sterile air which has been consumed.
UAWSBrew:TemperatureColdWort: This data point gives the current cold wort temperature at the wort plate heat exchanger.
UAWSBasis:Temperature: This data point gives the current temperature.
UAWSBrew:TemperatureRunback: This data point gives the temperature of the runback.

UAWSBrew:LifetimeSterileFilter: This data point gives the accumulated value of batches running through filter plates/sheets. This point helps to determine the lifetime of filter sheets/cloth as a measure for preventive maintenance.
UAWSBrew:CounterNormalBatches: This data point gives the counter value of the total amount of produced batches within set/normal quality specifications.
UAWSBasis:VolumeFlow: This data point gives the current volume flow.
UAWSBrew:QuantityColdWort: This data point defines the quantity of cold wort, leaving the cooling unit/ brewhouse unit.
UAWSBrew:QuantityCastWort: This data point defines the quantity of cast wort, leaving the wort kettle unit.
UAWSBrew:ExtractProduct: This data point gives the amount of extract in the product.
UAWSBasis:pHValueProduct: This data point gives the current pH-value of the product during an operation/unit procedure.
UAWSBrew:QuantityYeast: This data point defines the quantity of yeast being harvested in the cellar process cell/ units.
UAWSBrew:QuantityColdWater: This data point defines the used quantity of cold water in process cells.
UAWSBrew:QuantityIceWater: This data point defines the used quantity of ice water during wort cooling.
UAWSBrew:ProductColor: This data point gives the measured color of a suspension or a product.
UAWSBrew:InternationalBitterUnits: This data point gives the amount of international bitter units (IBU) of semi- finished product or product.

UAObjectType.Description: This ObjectType defines the type of flotation tanks used in the brewing process. A flotation tank is used to separate suspended solids and impurities from liquids by introducing gas bubbles that carry particles to the surface for removal.

UAWSBrew:O2inProduct: This data point gives the oxygen content of the product.

UAWSBrew:MicrobiologyTestResult: This data point implies the microbiological test result of a batch or product. Bits or integer must be used for batches with the following batch quality states: Table 8: Coding of microbiological test results Microbiology Test Results Integer coding Undefined/Blank 0 Negative 1 Within normal limits 2 Positive 4
UAWSBrew:YeastCellCounting: This data point gives the yeast cell count of a suspension or a product.

UAObjectType.Description: This ObjectType defines the type of yeast storage tanks used in the brewing process. A yeast storage tank is designed to store yeast safely before use in fermentation.

UAObjectType.Description: This ObjectType defines the type of centrifuges used in the brewing process. A centrifuge is a device that separates solids from liquids by spinning at high speeds, used for clarifying wort or beer.

Extension.uawsbrew:ImplementationNote: UAMachineryEM:Electricity: This data point gives the consumption of electricity.
UAWSBrew:CompressedAir: This data point gives the consumption of compressed air.
UAWSBrew:Steam: This data point gives the amount of steam which has been used.
UAWSBrew:HotWater: This data point gives the amount of hot water which has been consumed.
UAWSBrew:DeaeratedWater: This data point gives the amount of deaerated water which has been consumed.
UAWSBrew:CO2inProduct: This data point gives the CO2 content of the product.
UAWSBrew:O2inProduct: This data point gives the oxygen content of the product.
UAWSBasis:DifferentialPressure: This data point gives the current differential pressure. This point can also be used to log the transmembrane pressure.
UAWSBasis:TurbidityInlet: This data point gives the current turbidity of a suspension or a product at the inlet of a unit.
UAWSBasis:TurbidityOutlet: This data point gives the current turbidity of a suspension or a product at the outlet of a unit.
UAWSBasis:VolumeFlow: This data point gives the current volume flow.
UAWSBrew:ExtractProduct: This data point gives the amount of extract in the product.
UAWSBasis:Pressure: This data point gives the current pressure.
UAWSBasis:Temperature: This data point gives the current temperature.
UAWSBrew:QuantityExtract: This data point defines the quantity of extract per batch, brewhouse line, process cell, timeframe.
UAWSBrew:CounterNormalBatches: This data point gives the counter value of the total amount of produced batches within set/normal quality specifications.
UAWSBrew:CounterdeficientBatches: This data point gives the counter value of the total amount of deficient batches. Deficient batches do not apply to the companies’ quality specifications.

UAWSBrew:CountVolumeFilterSheets: This data point gives the accumulated volume of product running through filter plates/sheets. This point helps to determine the lifetime of filter sheets/cloth as a measure for preventive maintenance.
UAWSBrew:Revolutions: This data point gives the current revolutions of a unit.
UAMachineryProcessValues:ProcessValueSetpoint: This data point gives the set blending rate during blending processes during, e.g. filtrations.
UAMachineryProcessValues:ProcessValueSetpoint: This data point gives the set dilution factor during dilution of high-gravity wort or (fermented) products.

UAMachineryProcessValues:ProcessValueSetpoint: This data point gives the set body feed of filter aids to a running filter process. The point can be used for all kinds of dosed filter aids and stabilization aids.
UAWSBrew:YeastCellCounting: This data point gives the yeast cell count of a suspension or a product.

UAWSBrew:MicrobiologyTestResult: This data point implies the microbiological test result of a batch or product. Bits or integer must be used for batches with the following batch quality states: Table 8: Coding of microbiological test results Microbiology Test Results Integer coding Undefined/Blank 0 Negative 1 Within normal limits 2 Positive 4
UAWSBrew:ProductColor: This data point gives the measured color of a suspension or a product.

UAWSBasis:OperatingMode: The operating mode provides information about the nature and extent of the intervention on the control equipment by the operators, and also via feedback from the equipment (DIN 19 237).

UAWSBasis:PackMLBaseStateMachine: The PackMLBaseStateMachine maps the state of a WS Machine according to the PackML state machine. The PackML state machine is extended to include sub-states of the Weihenstephan Standards.

UAObjectType.Description: This ObjectType defines the type of sheet filters used in the brewing process. A sheet filter uses filter sheets or pads to remove suspended solids and haze from beer or wort.

UAWSBasis:OperatingMode: The operating mode provides information about the nature and extent of the intervention on the control equipment by the operators, and also via feedback from the equipment (DIN 19 237).

UAWSBasis:PackMLBaseStateMachine: The PackMLBaseStateMachine maps the state of a WS Machine according to the PackML state machine. The PackML state machine is extended to include sub-states of the Weihenstephan Standards.
UAMachineryEM:Electricity: This data point gives the consumption of electricity.
UAWSBrew:CompressedAir: This data point gives the consumption of compressed air.
UAWSBrew:HotWater: This data point gives the amount of hot water which has been consumed.
UAWSBrew:CO2: This data point gives the consumption of CO2.
UAWSBrew:Additive: This data point gives the consumption of the main additive which have been used.
UAWSBrew:DeaeratedWater: This data point gives the amount of deaerated water which has been consumed.

UAWSBrew:CountBatchesperFilterCloth: This data point gives the accumulated value of batches running through filter plates/sheets. This point helps to determine the lifetime of filter sheets/cloth as a measure for preventive maintenance.
UAWSBrew:ExtractProduct: This data point gives the amount of extract in the product.
UAWSBrew:CO2inProduct: This data point gives the CO2 content of the product.
UAWSBrew:O2inProduct: This data point gives the oxygen content of the product.
UAWSBasis:DifferentialPressure: This data point gives the current differential pressure. This point can also be used to log the transmembrane pressure.
UAWSBasis:TurbidityInlet: This data point gives the current turbidity of a suspension or a product at the inlet of a unit.
UAWSBasis:TurbidityOutlet: This data point gives the current turbidity of a suspension or a product at the outlet of a unit.

UAWSBrew:FilterAidFeed: This data point gives the current feed value of filter aids or stabilizing agent. If the consumption of a specific filter aid or stabilizing agent has to be measured, the data point can be extended with a number at position XX.
UAWSBasis:Pressure: This data point gives the current pressure.
UAWSBasis:VolumeFlow: This data point gives the current volume flow.
UAWSBrew:CounterdeficientBatches: This data point gives the counter value of the total amount of deficient batches. Deficient batches do not apply to the companies’ quality specifications.
UAWSBrew:QuantityExtract: This data point defines the quantity of extract per batch, brewhouse line, process cell, timeframe.
UAWSBasis:Temperature: This data point gives the current temperature.
UAMachineryProcessValues:ProcessValueSetpoint: This data point gives the set blending rate during blending processes during, e.g. filtrations.
UAMachineryProcessValues:ProcessValueSetpoint: This data point gives the set dilution factor during dilution of high-gravity wort or (fermented) products.
UAWSBrew:AlcoholContentinProduct: This data point gives the current amount of alcohol in a semi-finished product or product.
UAWSBrew:YeastCellCounting: This data point gives the yeast cell count of a suspension or a product.
UAWSBrew:InternationalBitterUnits: This data point gives the amount of international bitter units (IBU) of semi- finished product or product.
UAWSBrew:ProductColor: This data point gives the measured color of a suspension or a product.

UAWSBrew:MicrobiologyTestResult: This data point implies the microbiological test result of a batch or product. Bits or integer must be used for batches with the following batch quality states: Table 8: Coding of microbiological test results Microbiology Test Results Integer coding Undefined/Blank 0 Negative 1 Within normal limits 2 Positive 4

UAObjectType.Description: This ObjectType defines the type of candle or cartridge filters used in the brewing process. These filters use replaceable candle-shaped or cartridge filter elements to remove fine particles and impurities from beer or wort.

UAObjectType.Description: This ObjectType defines the type of membrane filters used in the brewing process. Membrane filters use semi-permeable membranes to separate particles, microorganisms, and other impurities from beer or wort.

UAWSBasis:OperatingMode: The operating mode provides information about the nature and extent of the intervention on the control equipment by the operators, and also via feedback from the equipment (DIN 19 237).

UAWSBasis:PackMLBaseStateMachine: The PackMLBaseStateMachine maps the state of a WS Machine according to the PackML state machine. The PackML state machine is extended to include sub-states of the Weihenstephan Standards.
UAMachineryEM:Electricity: This data point gives the consumption of electricity.
UAWSBrew:CompressedAir: This data point gives the consumption of compressed air.
UAWSBrew:Steam: This data point gives the amount of steam which has been used.
UAWSBrew:HotWater: This data point gives the amount of hot water which has been consumed.
UAWSBrew:CO2: This data point gives the consumption of CO2.
UAWSBrew:Additive: This data point gives the consumption of the main additive which have been used.
UAWSBrew:DeaeratedWater: This data point gives the amount of deaerated water which has been consumed.

UAObject.Description: This data point gives the consumption of electricity.
This data point gives the consumption of compressed air.
This data point gives the amount of steam which has been used.
This data point gives the amount of hot water which has been consumed.
This data point gives the consumption of CO2.
This data point gives the consumption of the main additive which have been used.
This data point gives the amount of deaerated water which has been consumed.

Extension.uawsbrew:ImplementationNote: UAWSBrew:ExtractProduct: This data point gives the amount of extract in the product.
UAWSBrew:CO2inProduct: This data point gives the CO2 content of the product.
UAWSBrew:O2inProduct: This data point gives the oxygen content of the product.
UAWSBasis:DifferentialPressure: This data point gives the current differential pressure. This point can also be used to log the transmembrane pressure.
UAWSBasis:TurbidityInlet: This data point gives the current turbidity of a suspension or a product at the inlet of a unit.
UAWSBasis:TurbidityOutlet: This data point gives the current turbidity of a suspension or a product at the outlet of a unit.

UAWSBrew:FilterAidFeed: This data point gives the current feed value of filter aids or stabilizing agent. If the consumption of a specific filter aid or stabilizing agent has to be measured, the data point can be extended with a number at position XX.
UAWSBasis:Pressure: This data point gives the current pressure.
UAWSBasis:VolumeFlow: This data point gives the current volume flow.
UAWSBrew:CounterdeficientBatches: This data point gives the counter value of the total amount of deficient batches. Deficient batches do not apply to the companies’ quality specifications.
UAWSBasis:Temperature: This data point gives the current temperature.
UAWSBasis:BypassFlowRate: This data point gives the flow rate of a bypass pipeline in, e.g. a filter or purification plant.
UAMachineryProcessValues:ProcessValueSetpoint: This data point gives the set blending rate during blending processes during, e.g. filtrations.
UAMachineryProcessValues:ProcessValueSetpoint: This data point gives the set dilution factor during dilution of high-gravity wort or (fermented) products.
UAWSBrew:AlcoholContentinProduct: This data point gives the current amount of alcohol in a semi-finished product or product.
UAWSBrew:YeastCellCounting: This data point gives the yeast cell count of a suspension or a product.
UAWSBrew:InternationalBitterUnits: This data point gives the amount of international bitter units (IBU) of semi- finished product or product.
UAWSBrew:ProductColor: This data point gives the measured color of a suspension or a product.

UAWSBrew:MicrobiologyTestResult: This data point implies the microbiological test result of a batch or product. Bits or integer must be used for batches with the following batch quality states: Table 8: Coding of microbiological test results Microbiology Test Results Integer coding Undefined/Blank 0 Negative 1 Within normal limits 2 Positive 4

UAObjectType.Description: This ObjectType defines the type of bright beer tanks used in the brewing process. A bright beer tank is a vessel where beer is clarified, carbonated, and stored before packaging.

UAObjectType.Description: This ObjectType defines the type of premix plants used in the brewing process. A premix plant is a system where ingredients like malt extract, water, and other additives are mixed and prepared before brewing or packaging.

Extension.uawsbrew:ImplementationNote: UAWSBrew:DeaeratedWater: This data point gives the amount of deaerated water which has been consumed.
UAWSBasis:pHValueProduct: This data point gives the current pH-value of the product during an operation/unit procedure.
UAWSBasis:VolumeFlow: This data point gives the current volume flow.

UAWSBrew:O2inProduct: This data point gives the oxygen content of the product.
UAWSBasis:Temperature: This data point gives the current temperature.
UAWSBrew:QuantityBeverage: This data point defines the produced quantity of beverage in a premix plant.
UAWSBrew:QuantityCompound: This data point defines the used quantity of compound in premix plants.
UAWSBrew:CountTotalBatches: This data point gives the counter value of the total amount of produced batches.

UAWSBrew:QuantitySugar: This data point defines the used quantity of sugar in a process cell.
UAWSBrew:TotalQuantity: This data point counts the total product quantity of a process cell.

UAObjectType.Description: This ObjectType defines the type of flash pasteurization units used in the brewing process. Flash pasteurization rapidly heats beer or other liquids to a specific temperature for a short time to kill microorganisms and extend shelf life, then quickly cools it down.

Extension.uawsbrew:ImplementationNote: UAMachineryEM:Electricity: This data point gives the consumption of electricity.
UAWSBrew:Steam: This data point gives the amount of steam which has been used.
UAWSBrew:SterileAir: This data point gives the amount of sterile air which has been consumed.
UAWSBasis:Temperature: This data point gives the current temperature.
UAWSBrew:TemperatureRunback: This data point gives the temperature of the runback.
UAWSBrew:QuantityColdWater: This data point defines the used quantity of cold water in process cells.
UAWSBasis:VolumeFlow: This data point gives the current volume flow.
UAWSBrew:QuantityYeast: This data point defines the quantity of yeast being harvested in the cellar process cell/ units.
UAWSBrew:QuantityColdWort: This data point defines the quantity of cold wort, leaving the cooling unit/ brewhouse unit.
UAWSBrew:ConductanceProduct: This data point gives the product conductance.
UAWSBrew:QuantityCastWort: This data point defines the quantity of cast wort, leaving the wort kettle unit.
UAWSBrew:ExtractProduct: This data point gives the amount of extract in the product.
UAWSBrew:PasteurizationUnits: This data point gives the current pasteurization units.
UAWSBrew:ProductColor: This data point gives the measured color of a suspension or a product.
